---
title: "color_peer_factor_by_known_factors"
author: "Crystal Shan"
date: "2025-07-29"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r load}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(DESeq2)
library(BiocParallel)
library(magrittr)
library(stringr)
library(gplots)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggfortify)
library(reactable)
library(gridExtra)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(tidyr)
library(tibble)
Dir="~/Downloads/nl/human/skin/eQTLs/RNA-Seq/peer/"
load(file=paste0(Dir,"/myEnvironment_main.RData"))
```

```{r compile}
# peer factor
dir.peer <- "~/Downloads/nl/human/skin/eQTLs/RNA-Seq/peer/peer_factors"
sc <- "MEL_PBS-IFNG"
peer_factors <- data.table::fread(paste0(dir.peer, "/peer_factors_",sc,".tsv")) 
colnames(peer_factors)[1] <- "sample"

# genotype PC
genotype_pcs <- data.table::fread("~/Downloads/nl/human/skin/eQTLs/donor_genotyping/hg38/genotype_PCs_for_modeling_07242025.txt") %>% 
  dplyr::select(-c("FID","donor_num")) 

# metadata
this_df <- left_join(peer_factors, metadata, by="sample") %>%
  left_join( . , genotype_pcs, by="donor")

# reshape to long format, restrict to peer1 to 10
peer_cols <- grep("^PEER", names(this_df), value = TRUE)  # detect PEER columns
peer_cols <- paste0("PEER", 1:10)
pc_cols <- paste0("PC", 1:10)

# STEP 2: Convert read_length to numeric
this_df$read_length_bp <- as.numeric(gsub("bp", "", this_df$read_length))

this_long <- this_df %>%
  select(all_of(peer_cols), sequencing_platform, total_reads, read_length, all_of(pc_cols)) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(peer_cols), names_to = "PEER", values_to = "value")
this_long$PEER <- factor(this_long$PEER, levels = paste0("PEER", 1:10))

```
```{r plot}
# Plot PEER factors vs sequencing_platform (categorical variable)
p.seqplatform <- ggplot(this_long, aes(x = sequencing_platform, y = value, fill = sequencing_platform)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.size = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~ PEER, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(title = "PEER Factors vs Sequencing Platform",
       y = "PEER Value", x = "Sequencing Platform") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# Plot PEER factors vs total_reads (continuous variable)
p.totalread <- ggplot(this_long, aes(x = total_reads, y = value)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "steelblue") +
  facet_wrap(~ PEER, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(title = "PEER Factors vs Total Reads",
       y = "PEER Value", x = "Total Reads")

# plot PEER vs read_length
this_df$read_length_bp <- as.numeric(gsub("bp", "", this_df$read_length))
this_long2 <- this_df %>%
  select(all_of(peer_cols), read_length_bp) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(peer_cols), names_to = "PEER", values_to = "value")

p.readlength <- ggplot(this_long2, aes(x = read_length_bp, y = value)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "darkgreen") +
  facet_wrap(~ PEER, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "PEER Factors vs Read Length",
       x = "Read Length (bp)", y = "PEER Value")

# PEER vs genotype PC
# Select only PEER and PC columns
library(pheatmap)
peer_cols <- paste0("PEER", 1:10)
pc_cols <- paste0("PC", 1:10)

this_df <- as.data.frame(this_df)
# Compute the correlation matrix using the correct data.table syntax
cor_matrix <- cor(this_df[, peer_cols], this_df[, pc_cols], use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "pearson")

# Plot
p.genotypePC <- pheatmap(cor_matrix,
         main = "Correlation: PEER factors vs Genotype PCs",
         cluster_rows = TRUE,
         cluster_cols = TRUE,
         display_numbers = TRUE,
         fontsize_number = 10)
```
```{r cormat}
library(data.table)
library(Hmisc)  # for rcorr()

# Define your PEER and covariate columns
peer_cols <- paste0("PEER", 1:10)
known_covariates <- setdiff(colnames(this_df), c("sample", peer_cols))  # exclude sample name and PEERs

# Initialize results list
cor_results <- list()

# Loop over PEER factors and known covariates
for (peer in peer_cols) {
  for (covar in known_covariates) {
    print(paste0(peer,"_",covar))
    x <- this_df[[peer]]
    y <- this_df[[covar]]
    
    # Only do correlation if both are numeric
    if (is.numeric(x) && is.numeric(y)) {
      result <- cor.test(x, y, method = "pearson")
      cor_results[[paste(peer, covar, sep = "_vs_")]] <- list(
        peer = peer,
        covariate = covar,
        cor = result$estimate,
        pval = result$p.value
      )
    }
  }
}

# Convert list to data.frame
cor_df <- rbindlist(cor_results, fill = TRUE)

# Adjust p-values (optional)
cor_df[, p_adj := p.adjust(pval, method = "fdr")]

cor_heatmap <- ggplot(cor_df, aes(x = peer, y = covariate, fill = -log10(p_adj))) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "plasma", direction = -1) +
  labs(title = "Covariate correlation matrix\n lm() CPM ~ covariates + genotype * condition + (1|donor)", fill = "-log10(p)") +
  theme_minimal()

# View top hits
cor_df[order(pval)][1:20]

#### ------ Heatmap --------- ####
library(ComplexHeatmap)
library(circlize)

# Filter and pivot to wide matrix
mat <- cor_df %>%
  select(peer, covariate, cor) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = covariate, values_from = cor) %>%
  column_to_rownames("peer") %>%
  as.matrix()

# Create custom color function
col_fun <- colorRamp2(
  c(-1, -0.5, 0, 0.5, 1),
  c("darkblue", "lightblue", "white", "lightcoral", "darkred")
)

# Plot
p <- Heatmap(
  mat,
  name = "cor",
  col = col_fun,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_columns = FALSE,
  heatmap_legend_param = list(title = "Correlation")
)
```
